data.description:

Evergreen Lithium Ltd. ( (AU:EG1) ) has shared an announcement.

Evergold Minerals Limited has announced the cessation of 500,000 options with an exercise price of $0.40, which expired unexercised on 11 April 2026. The expiry of these options reduces the company’s pool of potential dilutive securities, slightly simplifying its capital structure but having no immediate impact on current share count.

The change was disclosed via an Appendix 3H notification to the ASX, confirming the updated status of the company’s issued capital. This administrative update provides clarity for investors and ensures the market has accurate information on the number and type of Evergold’s outstanding securities.

More about Evergreen Lithium Ltd.

Evergold Minerals Limited is an ASX-listed resources company focused on mineral exploration and development. The company’s activities center on identifying and advancing mineral assets, with its securities trading under the ticker EG1 on the Australian Securities Exchange.

data.title: Audioboom Posts Record 2025 Results as Video and UK Expansion Drive Growth
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Audioboom ( (GB:BOOM) ) is now available.

Audioboom Group reported record audited results for 2025, with revenue rising 10% to US$80.4 million and gross profit up 17% to US$16.9 million, driven by a focus on higher-quality revenue and platform efficiency. Annual adjusted EBITDA climbed 54% to US$5.1 million, ahead of market expectations, while Q4 delivered record quarterly revenue and margins, and the group ended the year with improved cash and the final resolution of onerous contracts, restoring adjusted EBITDA as a proxy for cash generation.

Operationally, the company accelerated growth in its higher-margin Showcase marketplace, expanded into leadership in video podcasting, and completed the acquisition and rapid integration of UK podcast network Adelicious, creating the country’s second-largest network. Early 2026 trading has been strong, with record Q1 revenue and EBITDA, new commercial and technical partnerships with Spotify and Apple to bolster video monetisation, renewed key creator contracts and new tier-one content deals, all reinforcing Audioboom’s positioning for further consolidation and growth in the global podcast market.

Spark’s Take on BOOM Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BOOM is a Neutral.

Audioboom’s overall stock score is driven by strong technical indicators and positive corporate events, offset by challenges in cash flow generation and a high P/E ratio. The strategic review and record Q3 performance highlight potential growth opportunities, while the stock’s upward trend supports a positive outlook.

To see Spark’s full report on BOOM stock, click here.

More about Audioboom

Audioboom Group is a global podcasting company that operates a large-scale ad-tech and monetisation platform for premium podcasts. The group provides commercial, distribution, marketing and production services to top-tier shows, and distributes content across major platforms including Apple Podcasts, YouTube, Spotify and other global streaming services.

Average Trading Volume: 68,833

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: £113.3M

data.title: Boku Tightens Free Float with New Share Buyback on AIM
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

BOKU ( (GB:BOKU) ) has issued an update.

Boku Inc., a London-based provider of local payment method networks and value-added services such as bundling and cross-border settlement, supports global technology, media and entertainment merchants with access to direct carrier billing, digital wallets and real-time account-to-account payments. Its platform connects merchants to over 7 billion consumer payment accounts, enabling streamlined integration to local payment methods and fraud prevention.

The company has repurchased 160,245 of its own ordinary shares on AIM between 13 and 15 April 2026 via Investec Bank, at volume-weighted average prices around 173 pence, and will hold these shares in treasury under Diagonal Nominees Ltd. Following these transactions, Boku’s issued share capital stands at 303,738,417 shares, of which 6,658,664 are in treasury and 297,079,753 carry voting rights, a change that slightly reduces the free float and adjusts the denominator for shareholders’ disclosure obligations under FCA rules.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:BOKU) stock is a Buy with a £311.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on BOKU stock, see the GB:BOKU Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on BOKU Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BOKU is a Neutral.

The score is primarily supported by strong financial performance (re-accelerating growth, strong cash flow, and low leverage). Offsetting this are weak technical trends (trading below major moving averages with negative MACD) and a high P/E valuation that increases execution risk.

To see Spark’s full report on BOKU stock, click here.

More about BOKU

Boku Inc. is a London-headquartered fintech operating a global network of local payment methods used by major technology, media and entertainment companies. Through a single integration, it offers merchants access to direct carrier billing, digital wallets and real-time account-to-account schemes, alongside bundling, currency conversion and cross-border settlement services.

The company’s platform connects merchants to more than 7 billion consumer payment accounts worldwide, helping them acquire paying users, simplify integration with hundreds of local payment options and reduce fraud. Founded in 2008, Boku has expanded its presence with offices across Europe, the Americas and Asia, supporting international expansion for its global client base.

Average Trading Volume: 946,417

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: £511.3M

data.title: Nanshan Aluminium Plans Governance Revamp for Digital Shareholder Participation
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Nanshan Aluminium International Holdings Limited ( (HK:2610) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Nanshan Aluminium International Holdings Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, operates under Hong Kong’s listing rules and maintains a board structure that includes executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors. Its governance practices are influenced by the exchange’s Core Shareholder Protection Standards, which increasingly emphasise virtual participation and modernised shareholding arrangements.

The company plans to replace its current articles of association with a new version to align with updated shareholder protection standards, enable virtual general meetings with electronic voting, and prepare for an uncertificated securities regime allowing shares to be held and transferred in electronic form. These proposed amendments, which also include technical housekeeping updates, require approval by special resolution at the upcoming annual general meeting and signal a further shift toward digital corporate governance and streamlined share administration for investors.

More about Nanshan Aluminium International Holdings Limited

Nanshan Aluminium International Holdings Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the aluminium sector and is governed by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors. The company is subject to the Rules Governing the Listing of Securities on The Stock Exchange of Hong Kong, which shape its corporate governance and shareholder protection framework.

The firm’s listing status requires alignment with evolving regulatory standards, including provisions for virtual shareholder participation and electronic shareholding structures. These governance adjustments reflect broader market moves toward digitisation of securities and enhanced investor rights in Hong Kong’s capital markets.

Average Trading Volume: 3,084,647

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$28.66B

Light & Wonder, Inc. Shs Chess Depository Interests Repr 1 Sh ( (AU:LNW) ) has provided an announcement.

Light & Wonder Inc. has notified the market of the issuance of unquoted equity securities under its employee incentive schemes. The company will issue 118,600 performance restricted stock units and 1,211,191 restricted stock units, both dated March 31, 2026, which are subject to transfer restrictions and will not be quoted on the ASX until those restrictions end.

The new securities are part of Light & Wonder’s broader approach to employee compensation and retention using equity incentives. As these units are unquoted and restricted, the move primarily affects internal capital structure and staff incentives rather than immediately altering the tradable float available to public shareholders.

data.description:

The latest announcement is out from Mitsubishi Estate Logistics REIT Investment Corp. ( (JP:3481) ).

Mitsubishi Estate Logistics REIT Investment Corporation announced that its asset management company, Mitsubishi Jisho Investment Advisors, will reorganize its corporate structure to strengthen business planning, including the creation of a New Business Development Department. The reorganization, effective June 1, 2026, is designed to enhance the sophistication of the firm’s planning capabilities while remaining compliant with Japanese financial and real estate regulations, signaling a strategic move to support future growth in its logistics-focused real estate portfolio.

The asset manager plans to refine internal functions and procedures as part of this organizational change, reflecting a focus on more advanced planning in response to market and regulatory demands. These measures are expected to bolster the REIT’s ability to identify and pursue new business opportunities in the logistics real estate sector, potentially improving operational efficiency and reinforcing its competitive positioning in Japan’s logistics property market.

More about Mitsubishi Estate Logistics REIT Investment Corp.

Mitsubishi Estate Logistics REIT Investment Corporation is a Japanese real estate investment trust focused on logistics facilities, managed by Mitsubishi Jisho Investment Advisors, Inc. The REIT operates within Japan’s commercial real estate market, emphasizing logistics assets that support distribution, warehousing, and related supply chain needs for a range of corporate tenants.

Irish Residential Properties REIT ( (GB:0QT8) ) has provided an update.

Irish Residential Properties REIT has published its Annual Report and Accounts for the year ended 31 December 2025, alongside its 2025 Sustainability Report, and issued the notice convening its 2026 Annual General Meeting. The AGM is scheduled for 28 May 2026 at The Spencer Hotel in Dublin, with all key documents now accessible on the company’s website and submitted to Euronext Dublin for regulatory inspection.

The release underscores IRES’s ongoing emphasis on transparency and sustainability reporting, providing shareholders and other stakeholders with detailed financial and non-financial disclosures ahead of the AGM. data.title: EasyJet warns of deeper first-half loss as fuel shock offsets resilient demand
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

EasyJet ( (GB:EZJ) ) has shared an update.

EasyJet expects a headline pre-tax loss of £540–£560 million for the first half of FY26, as higher fuel costs linked to the Middle East conflict and an increase in legal provisions offset solid demand and a 90% load factor. The airline’s strategic capacity investments in Milan Linate and Rome Fiumicino, along with strong growth at EasyJet holidays, supported a 3% rise in second-quarter revenue per seat despite war-related softness in Egypt, Turkey and Cyprus.

Unit costs excluding fuel rose about 8% in the first half, inflated by resilience measures, higher airport charges and digital investments, though management anticipates only low single-digit growth in these costs in the second half. While recent geopolitical tensions have shortened the booking curve and introduced fuel-price uncertainty, EasyJet highlights its investment-grade balance sheet, £4.7 billion of liquidity and high aircraft ownership as key buffers that leave it well positioned to manage volatility and pursue its medium-term financial targets.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:EZJ) stock is a Sell with a £3.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on EasyJet stock, see the GB:EZJ Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on EZJ Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, EZJ is a Neutral.

The score is supported primarily by improving profitability and a solid balance sheet, plus an attractive valuation (low P/E and decent dividend yield). These positives are meaningfully offset by very weak technicals, with the stock far below key moving averages and strong bearish momentum indicators.

To see Spark’s full report on EZJ stock, click here.

More about EasyJet

EasyJet is a U.K.-based low-cost airline group focused on short-haul European travel, operating both a core airline business and the fast-growing EasyJet holidays package division. The company targets value-conscious leisure and city-break customers across key markets including the U.K., Italy and the wider Western Mediterranean region.

Average Trading Volume: 6,223,750

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: £2.96B
